name: Green Apple Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean sterilized jelly bag and cheesecloth. Do not peel or core the fruit; just wash and cut into pieces. Seeds and skin contain most of the pectin, so they should be cooked along with the pulp to ensure a firm jelly.
2. Cover the fruit with water, adding more if needed. Add lemon juice and bring to a boil until tender.
3. Remove from heat and strain the fruit through the jelly bag.
4. Do not attempt to rush the juice by squeezing the bag or mashing the fruit; this will cause the jelly to be cloudy.
5. Let the fruit stand in the jelly bag for 4 to 6 hours, or overnight if space is available.
6. The following morning, add sugar at a rate of 1 cup per cup of juice. Bring to a boil until a candy thermometer reads 220 degrees F, then turn off the heat and skim the froth.
7. Fill and cap jars. Ensure your pan is large enough for the jelly to reach a rolling boil without being stirred down; the jelly must boil at a vigorous temperature until it reaches the 220-degree stage or it will not set.
